items.title: Robodebt inquiry clears Scott Morrison but finds two others corrupt
items.summary: A watchdog's probe into officials involved in the robodebt scheme found two people carried out corrupt conduct.

items.title: Scouring the countryside for 600L of fuel: Farmer Rob has 'never seen' anything like this
items.summary: Farmers warn supply disruptions could threaten Australia's food production.

items.title: Qantas hikes international fares as war sends fuel costs soaring
items.summary: Despite the war, high demand for international flights has seen cheaper fares selling much faster than usual.

items.title: 'Patchwork' disaster response criticised as Aboriginal communities submerged in rising floodwaters
items.summary: The NT's peak Aboriginal legal body has put their emergency measures to the Commonwealth and Territory government as Minister McCarthy called out the lack of mainstream media flood coverage on national television.

items.title: Sonja asked high schoolers to debate about tradwives — and received international backlash
items.summary: In Australia, are terms such as "free speech" and "cultural safety" now used in place of constructive, respectful debate?

items.title: Iranian footballer reverses asylum decision as country's football chief criticises Australia — as it happened
items.summary: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said officials at the Iranian embassy would collect the team member and her teammates would be relocated.

items.title: US and Israel launch 'most intense day of strikes' yet against Iran
items.summary: Tehran residents have described the war's heaviest night of bombardment.

items.title: 'She can't make a decision': Desperate plea amid fears Iranian players are being coerced
items.summary: After five players were granted asylum, concerns are mounting over whether the remaining team members can safely seek protection.

items.title: PM says Australia will deploy RAAF plane and missiles to Gulf to protect civilians
items.summary: The government has responded to a request for assistance by the United Arab Emirates, deploying a high-tech spy plane to the Middle East.

items.title: 'A disturbing picture': Australia's deadliest cancer is no longer lung cancer
items.summary: A fast-growing group of cancers has quietly surpassed lung cancer as Australia's biggest killer, according to a new analysis.

items.title: Phillip was a teenager when a devastating flood hit his community. Now it's happening again
items.summary: Phillip Morgan is one of almost a thousand residents that have been evacuated due to major flooding across the Northern Territory. It's the worst flooding seen since the catastrophic 1998 levels.

items.title: Iran says US talks 'not on the agenda', calls previous negotiations a 'bitter experience'
items.summary: The Iranian deputy foreign minister says numerous countries have urged Iran to consider a ceasefire.

items.title: 'Maximum damage': Iran's tactics in the face of 'overwhelming' US force
items.summary: Iran's retaliatory strikes reflect a strategy of survival, not victory, experts say, as war in the Middle East deepens.

items.title: How high could petrol prices rise? Eight ways to stretch your tank
items.summary: The average price of unleaded petrol soared past $2 a litre in many parts of the country. So how can drivers save?

items.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill nearly 400 as Tehran names new supreme leader
items.summary: As fighting escalated on day nine of the US-Israeli campaign against Iran, thick black smoke hung over Tehran on Sunday local time.

items.title: Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader?
items.summary: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba has succeeded his late father as supreme leader, in a sign that hardliners are still firmly in charge.

items.title: 'We've reached a breaking point': Is it time to tax older Australians more?
items.summary: Some Australians are richer than ever, while younger cohorts are struggling. Experts say it's time for change.

items.title: Matildas score late in draw with South Korea, losing top spot at Women’s Asian Cup
items.summary: Australia have survived a Women's Asian Cup scare, scoring a late goal to avoid defeat in a 3-3 draw with South Korea.

items.title: Government urged to let Iranian women's football team stay in Australia until safety is assured
items.summary: Several community and civil society groups have sent the home affairs minister a letter expressing "grave concerns" for the team.

items.title: What's causing the Greek yoghurt shortage in Australian supermarkets?
items.summary: Many shops are running low on Greek yoghurt. Has tzatziki suddenly become wildly popular, or is it the result of online trends?

items.title: When Janelle retired, she never guessed periods would be her new passion
items.summary: Crafter Janelle Dodd has built a community to educate and help people around the world with menstruation.

items.title: Australia considering military aid to Middle East, but won't deploy troops
items.summary: Iran's retaliatory strikes have hit the UAE, Qatar and Bahrain, among other countries.

items.title: 'People confuse China's policy': Behind Russian, Chinese view on war in the Middle East
items.summary: Russia, China and Iran have had close relationships for decades, but Beijing and Moscow have yet offered any direct support for Tehran.

items.title: Shantanu was shot during Nepal's gen Z protests. What's next for him — and his country?
items.summary: Shantanu set out to change Nepal. Instead, the gen Z protests changed him. Nepal will soon have a new government — but will it deliver real change?
